report_name: iRhythm Technologies Hit by Extortion Attack, Patient Health Data Stolen

threat_actors.name: Unidentified Threat Actor
threat_actors.description: An unidentified threat actor responsible for a social engineering attack on iRhythm Technologies' third-party business applications. The actor exfiltrated proprietary company data and patient Protected Health Information (PHI) and subsequently issued a ransom demand to prevent public disclosure.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through the theft and threatened release of sensitive data.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: The attack was initiated via a social engineering campaign, likely a phishing attack, to compromise credentials for third-party business applications.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor used compromised credentials, obtained through social engineering, to gain legitimate access to the targeted third-party business applications.

attack_patterns.name: Email Collection
attack_patterns.description: Once inside the compromised third-party application, the threat actor located and aggregated sensitive data, including patient PHI and proprietary company files for exfiltration. The article maps this to T1114, though the collection was from a business application rather than directly from mail servers.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor exfiltrated the collected proprietary data and patient PHI from the compromised third-party environment to their own attacker-controlled infrastructure.

identities.name: iRhythm Technologies
identities.description: A digital healthcare company and maker of the Zio wearable cardiac monitor that was the victim of a data breach. The breach resulted from a social engineering attack on its third-party applications, leading to the theft of patient PHI and a ransom demand.

infrastructure.name: Attacker Exfiltration Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Unspecified attacker-controlled infrastructure used to receive the exfiltrated data from iRhythm's compromised third-party applications.

indicators.name: Anomalous Data Export from Cloud Application
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Data Export from Cloud Application
id: 9c5b1f3a-7d8e-4b9f-8c7a-1b2d3e4f5a6b
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects potentially anomalous data export activity from cloud or SaaS applications by looking for keywords related to data export in URLs. This can be an indicator of data exfiltration from a compromised account.
references:
  - https://securityaffairs.com/193721/data-breach/irhythm-hit-by-cyberattack-patient-data-stolen-and-ransom-demanded.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-17
tags:
  - attack.t1567
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains:
      - '/export'
      - '?export='
      - 'download_all'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects high-volume GET or export requests in web traffic, which could indicate bulk data exfiltration from a compromised business application, as suggested in the article's hunting hints.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous Data Export from Cloud Application
